source.title: #004 Ivan Orlović (MOST): Reforma izbornog sustava, samoodrživost Hrvatske i banke - 9.4.2026.

summary.title_hr: Ivan Orlović (MOST): Reforma izbornog sustava, samoodrživost Hrvatske i banke
summary.abstract_hr: U ovoj epizodi podcasta Domovina TV, Ivan Orlović, organizacijski tajnik Mosta, analizira ključne političke i gospodarske teme u Hrvatskoj. Razgovor se fokusira na kritiku izbornog sustava, uključujući D'Hondtovu metodu i podjelu na izborne jedinice, te predlaže reforme za veću demokratičnost. Također se raspravlja o utjecaju stranog bankarskog sektora, važnosti samoodrživosti u proizvodnji hrane i energetici, te problemima malih poduzetnika. Orlović naglašava dosljednost Mosta i poziva građane na aktivno sudjelovanje u političkom životu.
key_topics: pasivnost birača i izlaznost na izbore
ideološke i egzistencijalne teme u politici
key_points: Ivan Orlović, organizacijski tajnik Mosta, opisuje svoju ulogu koja uključuje rukovođenje ljudstvom, koordinaciju ureda i odgovornost za financije i pravne akte stranke.
Kritizira se trenutni izborni sustav u Hrvatskoj, posebno D'Hondtova metoda i podjela na više izbornih jedinica, što dovodi do neproporcionalne raspodjele mandata.
Predlaže se reforma izbornog sustava uvođenjem jedne izborne jedinice za cijelu Hrvatsku, povećanjem broja preferencijalnih glasova i smanjenjem izbornog praga na tri posto.
Ističe se da vladajućim strankama (HDZ i SDP) odgovara postojeći izborni sustav jer im donosi dobre rezultate, te da su propuštene prilike za njegovu promjenu.

Raspravlja se o negativnom utjecaju stranog bankarskog sektora na hrvatske građane, sugerirajući da banke previše određuju sudbinu pojedinaca i da bi domaće banke mogle bolje služiti nacionalnim interesima.
Naglašava se važnost samoodrživosti Hrvatske u proizvodnji hrane i energetici, s obzirom na geopolitičku situaciju i neiskorištene potencijale, te se kritizira prevelik uvoz.
Poziva se na aktivno sudjelovanje birača u izborima, čak i poništavanjem listića, kako bi se izbjegla pasivnost i spriječile malverzacije, te kako bi se osiguralo da pravedni ljudi budu u Saboru.
mentioned_organizations: Hrvatska banka za obnovu i razvitak
summary.title_en: Ivan Orlović (MOST): Electoral system reform, Croatia's self-sustainability and banks
summary.abstract_en: In this episode of the Domovina TV podcast, Ivan Orlović, organizational secretary of Most, analyzes key political and economic topics in Croatia. The conversation focuses on the criticism of the electoral system, including the D'Hondt method and the division into electoral units, and proposes reforms for greater democracy. Also discussed is the influence of the foreign banking sector, the importance of self-sustainability in food production and energy, and the problems of small entrepreneurs. Orlović emphasizes the consistency of Most and calls on citizens for active participation in political life.
key_topics_en: passivity of voters and turnout at elections
ideological and existential themes in politics
key_points_en: Ivan Orlović, organizational secretary of Most, describes his role which includes managing personnel, coordinating offices and responsibility for the finances and legal acts of the party.
The current electoral system in Croatia is criticized, especially the D'Hondt method and the division into multiple electoral units, which leads to a disproportionate distribution of mandates.

A reform of the electoral system is proposed by introducing one electoral unit for the whole of Croatia, by increasing the number of preferential votes, and by reducing the electoral threshold to three percent.
It is emphasized that the existing electoral system suits the ruling parties (HDZ and SDP) because it brings them good results, and that opportunities for its change have been missed.

The negative impact of the foreign banking sector on Croatian citizens is discussed, suggesting that banks determine too much the fate of individuals and that domestic banks could better serve national interests.
The importance of Croatia's self-sustainability in food production and energy is emphasized, considering the geopolitical situation and untapped potentials, and excessive import is criticized.

There is a call for active participation of voters in elections, even by invalidating ballots, in order to avoid passivity and prevent malversations, and in order to ensure that righteous people are in the Parliament.
mentioned_organizations_en: Croatian Bank for Reconstruction and Development
